def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    rsi_window = params.get('rsi_window', 14)
    bollinger_window = params.get('bollinger_window', 20)
    bollinger_std = params.get('bollinger_std', 2)
    
    rsi = ta.momentum.rsi(df['close'], window=rsi_window).fillna(0)
    bollinger_lband = ta.volatility.bollinger_lband(df['close'], window=bollinger_window, window_dev=bollinger_std).fillna(0)
    bollinger_hband = ta.volatility.bollinger_hband(df['close'], window=bollinger_window, window_dev=bollinger_std).fillna(0)
    
    long_entry = (df['close'] < bollinger_lband) & (rsi < 30)
    short_entry = (df['close'] > bollinger_hband) & (rsi > 70)
    
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    signals[long_entry] = 1
    signals[short_entry] = -1
    
    return signals.fillna(0).astype(int)
rationale: Combines RSI oversold/overbought conditions with Bollinger Band extremes for USD/CHF mean-reversion signals, capitalizing on the pair's tendency to revert to mean after extreme moves.
